name: Tulip
description: Tulip (Tulipa spp.) is a popular spring bulb flower that is toxic to cats throughout all its parts, with the highest concentration of toxins in the bulbs. It contains tulipalin A and tulipalin B — allergenic lactone compounds — and other irritant glycosides. The bulb is particularly dangerous as it is accessible during planting and storage seasons.
safetyNotes: Toxic compounds: tulipalin A and tulipalin B (allergenic lactone compounds), along with irritant glycosides; bulbs most concentrated
Mechanism: tulipalins cause direct mucosal irritation and allergic contact dermatitis; large bulb ingestion may cause CNS depression and cardiac effects
Symptoms: vomiting, drooling, diarrhea, lethargy; large bulb ingestion may cause tremors, abnormal heart rate, or breathing difficulty
Bulbs are especially dangerous during autumn planting or spring storage — keep stored bulbs away from cats
Contact your vet if your cat ingests a bulb or shows more than mild GI symptoms

symptoms.name: Irritation to the mouth, throat, and skin, leading to profuse drooling (ptyalism)
symptoms.notes: This is typically a primary reaction to the allergenic lactones found in the plant12. 2. Vomiting and Diarrhea

symptoms.name: Vomiting, diarrhea, and significant stomach upset
symptoms.notes: These are the most common digestive symptoms3. Furthermore, if a cat ingests large, intact pieces of the tulip bulb, it can cause a physical intestinal obstruction8. 3. Depression

symptoms.name: Depression
symptoms.notes: This generalized symptom of toxicosis is noted alongside severe stomach upset68. 4. Elevated Heart Rate and Arrhythmias

symptoms.name: Elevated heart rate (tachycardia) and cardiac arrhythmias
symptoms.notes: Cardiovascular abnormalities typically present only after a cat has ingested larger amounts of the plant, especially the highly toxic bulb where the dangerous compounds are concentrated1.... 5. Respiratory Distress

symptoms.name: Changes to respiration and difficulty breathing
symptoms.notes: Similar to cardiac symptoms, respiratory distress is a severe systemic sign that occurs following a larger ingestion of the plant or its bulb1.... 6. Tremors and Convulsions

symptoms.name: Tremors and convulsions (seizures)
symptoms.notes: These severe neurological symptoms manifest when significant amounts of the plant, particularly the bulb containing the highest concentration of toxic alkaloids and lactones, are consumed
